channel.title: hiroto's things
channel.description: personal website with technical writing, tiny game demos, and photo-logs

item.title: cotrolling llm costs at scale
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description:
the real cost of llm use is already hitting some large companies like a truck. and i expect individual developers will soon start to feel the costs too.

i have been talking about the end of the subsidies and how to prepare for the actual costs of llms with my peers for a while now. and at the information and data team at cejam, i have helped us prepare for it in three major ways: implementing a governance gateway for cost control, providing training on llm usage, and deploying cheaper models.

item.title: a simple shared memory system for llms
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description:
i wanted an easy way to share context between sessions for the members of my team.

the basic first requirements i had for this system were:

  • crud: the ability to perform all 4 basic operations on the data
  • easy search: a simple, straight-forward way to search through the data so llms can easly retrieve relevant context

i did consider using a vector database, implementing a rag. but rags are not free to develop, run and maintain (talking mainly about infra costs here). i also think they would have been overkill1, a simple grep would sufice on well tagged data.

in my experience, llms work best with short, well structured information. if they are given too much room to halucinate, they will do it2.

the jsonl solution

  1. why go for simplicity when you can overcomplicate everything ↩

  2. reasons why i find the idea of an llm wiki so silly. the diagrams in the comments are silly too ↩

item.title: connecting to wifi via terminal with nmcli
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description:
went to sesc paulista. last time i visited was years ago, soon after it first opened. the top floor now seems to be accessible only through reservations now.

this was the first time i tried connecting to wifi with this new arch installation.

the cachyos distro came with nmcli. i am not yet certain that it is a common dependency for other distros.

this lists the available wifi networks:

nmcli device wifi list

this connects to wifi:

nmcli device wifi connect connection_name password connection_password

item.title: nikola post previews (teasers)
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description: took me a while, but i managed to figure out how to show a preview of a post instead of the whole post.

item.title: doing the lord's work with python and the walrus operator
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description:
(actual prod code for airflow)

[
    (
        a_data := get_users.partial(
            connection_meta=connection_meta
        ).expand(code=code_a)
    ),
    (
        b_data := get_users.partial(
            connection_meta=connection_meta
        ).expand(code=code_b)
    ),
] >> (fetch_content := content_fetch(a_data, b_data))

item.title: tiny thermal cam goes to masp
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description: last year, masp's new annex opened to the public. i had the opportunity to visit at opening week. a few weeks before that, i bought a tiny thermal camera that prints on receipt paper.

item.title: converting markdown with pandoc on arch
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description:
while using pandoc on arch, i got an error as i tried to convert a markdown to a pdf.

conversion snippet:

pandoc document.md -o document.pdf

item.title: is tsql's `between` inclusive?
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description:
microsoft's documentation for between doesn't mention inclusivity at all until section b, which suggests that between is inclusive. the information is only really explicitly stated all the way down in section d, where it states that (for datetime values, at least), between is inclusive.

however, as i have come to find out (not before a whole hour of debugging): it ... depends?

item.title: thoreau; walden; 31
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description: The finest qualities of our nature, like the bloom on fruits, can be preserved only by the most delicate handling. Yet we do not treat ourselves nor one another thus tenderly.

item.title: on nikola
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description: i tried using the auto builder, but either i did something wrong, or there is a bug with it. nikola depends on the python package watchdog for the continuous auto-build. i might try it again some other time.

item.title: setting up
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description:
looking at the commit history for the repository containing the source for this blog, you may notice that this was originally my personal website/cv.

my initial intention was to setup the present blog on a separate branch, but i forgot to switch branches before deleting everything.

in any case, i suppose i'll have to commit to this now. so here is a test post.

item.title: what to do when an airflow task inside a task group fails
item.dc:creator: rodigu
item.description:
checking if a task has failed on airflow is simple enough, and may be done with:

from airflow.operators.python import get_current_context

@task
def check_task_failed(task_id: str) -> bool:
      context = get_current_context()
      return (
          context["dag_run"]
            .get_task_instance(task_id=task_id)
            .state == State.FAILED
      )
